title: N.H. State Police announce unexpected death of 19-year veteran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sgt. Brian J. Ross, a 19-year veteran of New Hampshire State Police, died unexpectedly at age 41 in Greenland, NH.
Ross served in multiple roles, including as commander of the Collision Analysis and Reconstruction Unit.
Funeral services are scheduled for July 7; a fund is established for his wife and three children.

title: Jaylen Brown says Celtics showed ‘lack of respect’ after trade to Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized the Celtics for a lack of respect after being traded to the 76ers.
Brown expressed dissatisfaction with communication from Celtics President Brad Stevens regarding his unexpected trade.
The Celtics traded Brown to the 76ers for Paul George, two first-round picks, and two second-round picks.

title: Hull man dies after apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach, officials say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Robert Nolan, 76, drowned at Nantasket Beach on Wednesday evening, according to Plymouth County District Attorney Tim Cruz.
Nolan was found unresponsive, floating in the water, and pronounced dead around 8:40 p.m.
The Office of the Chief Medical Examiner is examining his body; no foul play is suspected.

title: Don Sweeney, Bruins’ retooling plans remain a work in progress
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney trades Joonas Korpisalo to Rangers for cap relief and acquires Will Borgen for draft picks.
Boston signs Connor Clifton, re-signs Jordan Harris; maintains $5.3 million cap space for potential future trades.
The Bruins focus on conservative roster changes amid shifting NHL dynamics, avoiding long-term high-cost contracts.

title: Parking will be free at state parks, beaches for Fourth of July weekend
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Parking fees at Massachusetts state parks and beaches are waived from July 3 to July 5 for the holiday.
The measure honors the 250th anniversary of the American Revolution, aiming to enhance public celebrations.
High-demand sites may close upon reaching capacity; residents urged to check DCR Park Alerts before visiting.

title: What’s up with those online ads for gas-saving gadgets?
author: John Paul
bullets: Online ads for gas-saving gadgets are generally ineffective according to John Paul, AAA Northeast's Car Doctor.
Run-flat tires provide safety benefits but still result in a harsher ride and higher replacement costs compared to conventional tires.
A timing belt in a 2002 Honda Accord should be replaced due to age, despite low mileage, as recommended by the Car Doctor.

title: Boston civilian board recommends changes to BPD policies on bodycam footage, federal entanglements
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The Office of Police Accountability and Transparency recommends changes to Boston Police's policies on bodycam footage release timelines.
OPAT urges Boston Police to limit sharing surveillance data with federal agencies to protect civil rights and community trust.
The board advises Boston Police to withdraw from federal task forces and reject certain DHS funds to enhance transparency.

title: Senators seek to restrict social media use for minors, target ‘most addictive features’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The Massachusetts Senate proposed a bill to limit addictive social media features for minors, such as autoplay and algorithmic feeds.
The legislation avoids mandatory age verification, asking the state attorney general to regulate age confirmation methods.
Minors will receive usage reminders, have limited nighttime notifications, and default privacy restrictions without needing government ID.

title: Isaiah Thomas comes to Jaylen Brown’s defense after shocking Celtics trade​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was traded from the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers in exchange for Paul George and four draft picks.
Amid criticism of Brown's performance, former teammate Isaiah Thomas defended his character on social media.
Brown expressed shock and disappointment at the trade, thanking Celtics fans and acknowledging his commitment to the team.

title: Jeremiah Poope takes us on the Freedom Trail for America’s 250th (yes, that’s really his name)
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Jeremiah Poope has been a Freedom Trail tour guide in Boston for 23 years.
Despite a heat wave, Poope wore full colonial garb for the tour on Boston Common.
He engages tourists with humor and personal anecdotes, enhancing the history experience.
